name: Blockchain Developer
oneLiner: Build and deploy blockchain protocols and decentralized applications (dApps) that run trustlessly on-chain.
dayInTheLife: You wake up, check GitHub for code reviews. Deploy a new contract feature to testnet. Debug a gas optimization issue via Hardhat. Attend a protocol meeting. Code a new feature in Rust for Solana. By evening, you've submitted 3 PRs and fixed 2 critical bugs.

juniorToSenior.junior: Can write basic smart contracts, understand EVM opcodes, deploy to testnet. Learning production best practices.
juniorToSenior.mid: Architecting DeFi protocols, optimizing gas, leading code reviews. Comfortable with multiple chains.
juniorToSenior.senior: Designing novel blockchain mechanisms, mentoring teams, contributing to protocol specifications. Authority in the space.

proofOfWorkTips: Deploy smart contracts on mainnet (even small ones) - show on-chain work
Contribute to open-source blockchain projects (Ethereum, Solana repos)
Build and ship a full dApp (frontend + contracts) - share on Twitter
Write technical blog posts explaining complex concepts
Participate in hackathons and submit winning projects

avgCompRange.note: Senior roles often include token grants (highly volatile, can be 50%+ of comp)

interviewQuestions: Explain the EVM and how gas optimization works. Walk us through a real optimization you made.
Design a simple DEX. What are the security considerations?
How would you handle reentrancy attacks? Show code examples.
Describe your experience with Solidity. What common pitfalls have you encountered?
Tell us about a blockchain project you built. What was the hardest part?

name: Smart Contract Developer (Solidity)
oneLiner: Write, test, audit, and deploy secure smart contracts that handle millions in value.
dayInTheLife: Review security audit report for your contracts. Optimize a function's gas by 40%. Write 15 new tests in Foundry. Debug a complex reentrancy scenario. Deploy to mainnet after 5 rounds of auditing. Check gas metrics and celebrate saving users $50k/month in fees.

juniorToSenior.junior: Writing basic Solidity, understanding ERC standards, can write tests. Learning security best practices.
juniorToSenior.mid: Architecting complex protocols, leading audits, teaching junior devs. Comfortable with multiple token standards.
juniorToSenior.senior: Designing DeFi primitives, formal verification, advising on protocol design. Published research-level work.

proofOfWorkTips: Deploy audited smart contracts on mainnet - link them on Twitter
Pass a formal security audit (even small contracts)
Contribute to Solidity language development or OpenZeppelin libraries
Write detailed technical posts on contract vulnerabilities you found
Win smart contract auditing competitions (e.g., Code4rena, Sherlock)

avgCompRange.note: Top auditors and protocol architects command premium. Token comp is significant.

interviewQuestions: What are the top 5 Solidity security vulnerabilities? How do you mitigate them?
Explain reentrancy. Show how to fix a vulnerable contract.
Design an ERC-20 token. What edge cases matter?
How do you optimize gas? Walk us through a complex optimization.
Tell us about a critical bug you found. How did you discover and fix it?

name: Rust Developer (e.g., for Solana)
oneLiner: Build high-performance blockchain programs on non-EVM chains like Solana using Rust.
dayInTheLife: Write a new instruction for your Solana program. Optimize memory usage and reduce compute units. Test on localnet. Debug a tricky borrow checker issue. Deploy to devnet. Code review PRs from team. Profile performance. Ship to mainnet. Check cluster stats.

juniorToSenior.junior: Learning Rust syntax, understanding Solana's model, writing basic programs. Hitting borrow checker errors.
juniorToSenior.mid: Architecting efficient programs, optimizing compute, teaching others Rust. Comfortable with Solana internals.
juniorToSenior.senior: Designing novel program architectures, mentoring, contributing to Anchor framework. Recognized expert.

proofOfWorkTips: Deploy Solana programs on mainnet - share on Twitter with code
Contribute to Solana ecosystem projects (Anchor, Pyth, Magic Eden)
Write Rust code that's public on GitHub - show clean, optimized code
Build a complete Solana dApp and ship it
Win Solana hackathons

avgCompRange.note: Rust devs are in high demand. Non-EVM chains pay competitively.

interviewQuestions: Explain Solana's architecture vs Ethereum. Why use Rust?
How do PDAs (Program Derived Addresses) work? Why are they important?
Write a simple Anchor program. What security considerations exist?
Tell us about a Rust bug you hit. How'd you debug it?
Design a Solana program for a lending protocol. Walk us through it.

name: Full Stack Web3 Developer
oneLiner: Handle frontend (React/Vue), backend, and blockchain integration to build complete dApps.
dayInTheLife: Build React components for your dApp. Connect wallet integration. Test Web3.js calls. Build backend APIs. Deploy frontend to Vercel. Debug contract integration issues. Review PRs from team. Optimize UX for gas costs. Deploy new features.

juniorToSenior.junior: Building UI components, connecting wallets, understanding Web3.js. Learning DeFi mechanics.
juniorToSenior.mid: Architecting full dApps, optimizing performance, leading frontend. Understanding contract interactions.
juniorToSenior.senior: Designing entire user experiences, mentoring teams, advising on product. Understanding DeFi deeply.

proofOfWorkTips: Build and ship a full dApp on mainnet - live demo matters
Create GitHub repos with clean, well-documented code
Write UX comparison posts (DeFi vs Web2)
Contribute to popular dApp front-ends (Uniswap, Aave, etc)
Win hackathons for best UX

avgCompRange.note: Varies based on company stage. Startups may offer more tokens.

interviewQuestions: Tell us about a full dApp you built. Walk through the architecture.
How do you optimize gas costs on the frontend?
Explain wallet integration. What security considerations matter?
How would you handle Web3.js errors gracefully?
Design a lending dApp frontend. What UX matters most?

name: DevOps Engineer
oneLiner: Manage infrastructure, CI/CD, and node operations for blockchain systems at scale.
dayInTheLife: Monitor node health dashboards. Scale Kubernetes clusters for load. Deploy new contract versions via CI/CD. Fix infrastructure issues. Optimize database performance. Set up monitoring alerts. On-call duty for critical issues. Review infrastructure changes.

juniorToSenior.junior: Setting up CI/CD, managing containers, learning blockchain nodes. Supervised deployments.
juniorToSenior.mid: Architecting infrastructure, optimizing node performance, mentoring. Complex deployments.
juniorToSenior.senior: Designing enterprise-scale infrastructure, leading team, advising on architecture. Zero-downtime deployments.

proofOfWorkTips: Set up production-grade CI/CD pipelines - show on GitHub
Run and maintain a blockchain node publicly - Etherscan or similar
Write infrastructure-as-code (Terraform) examples
Contribute to DevOps tools (Hardhat, Foundry plugins)
Blog about blockchain infrastructure challenges solved

avgCompRange.note: On-call and critical infrastructure roles pay premium.

interviewQuestions: Design infrastructure for a high-volume DeFi protocol.
How would you monitor blockchain node health?
Walk us through your CI/CD pipeline for smart contract deployments.
How do you handle zero-downtime deployments for blockchain systems?
Tell us about an infrastructure crisis you solved.

name: Smart Contract Auditor
oneLiner: Review code for critical vulnerabilities, security risks, and optimizations. Gate-keepers of safety.
dayInTheLife: Review 2,000 lines of smart contract code. Find 3 critical vulnerabilities and document them. Discuss fixes with dev team. Verify patches. Write detailed audit report. Review another project. Check bug bounty submissions. Help junior auditors level up.

juniorToSenior.junior: Finding known vulnerabilities, writing test cases, learning audit methodology. Supervised work.
juniorToSenior.mid: Leading audits, finding novel vulnerabilities, advising on design. 100+ audits under belt.
juniorToSenior.senior: Industry authority, publishing research, advising on protocol design, mentoring teams. Known name in the space.

proofOfWorkTips: Find bugs in live protocols and responsibly disclose - bounties pay well
Win Code4rena / Sherlock contests - public track record matters
Write detailed security analysis posts on found vulnerabilities
Contribute to Slither or other security tools
Pass formal security certifications

avgCompRange.note: Top auditors earn premium. Bounties can add $100k+/year.

interviewQuestions: What are the 10 most critical Solidity vulnerabilities?
Describe a complex vulnerability you found and how.
Walk us through your audit methodology for a lending protocol.
Explain formal verification. When is it necessary?
Tell us about a critical bug you found before the team knew.

name: Blockchain Security Engineer
oneLiner: Focus on cryptography, zero-knowledge proofs, and threat mitigation at the protocol level.
dayInTheLife: Research new cryptographic attacks. Design ZK-proof circuits. Threat model a new protocol. Review security specifications. Mentor security team. Publish security research. Attend crypto conferences. Advise on protocol upgrades.

juniorToSenior.junior: Learning cryptography, understanding ZK, studying protocols. Building foundations.
juniorToSenior.mid: Designing security solutions, leading threat modeling, teaching team. Publishing papers.
juniorToSenior.senior: Cryptography researcher, protocol advisor, industry thought leader. Setting standards.

proofOfWorkTips: Publish cryptographic research - academic papers matter
Contribute to ZK frameworks (circom, Cairo, Noir)
Speak at crypto conferences about security
Advise projects on security design
Win security research competitions
interviewQuestions: Explain zero-knowledge proofs. Design a ZK circuit for X.
How do you threat model a blockchain protocol?
What cryptographic primitives would you use for [scenario]?
Describe a protocol-level security bug you found.
What's the future of crypto security?

name: Crypto/On-Chain Analyst
oneLiner: Analyze blockchain data to uncover market trends, on-chain signals, and investment opportunities.
dayInTheLife: Write SQL query on Dune to track daily DEX volume. Find an emerging token pattern. Research its tokenomics. Publish findings on Twitter. Get 5k likes. Meet with trading team about the signal. Refine the query. Publish a detailed report. Present to stakeholders.

juniorToSenior.junior: Writing basic SQL queries, understanding blockchain metrics, learning on-chain analysis. Building dashboards.
juniorToSenior.mid: Creating novel metrics, discovering alpha signals, advising trading desks. Published research.
juniorToSenior.senior: Thought leader in on-chain analysis, publishing research, speaking at conferences. Authority in crypto.

proofOfWorkTips: Build public Dune dashboards - share constantly on Twitter
Publish on-chain analysis threads with actionable insights
Share alpha signals and be right - build a following
Create novel metrics nobody else is tracking
Contribute to token research databases

avgCompRange.note: Top analysts get trading firm bounties + salary. Consulting pays extra.

interviewQuestions: Show us your best on-chain analysis. How did you discover it?
Design a Dune dashboard to track [specific metric].
What on-chain signal do you think is misunderstood?
Tell us about a trading signal you spotted. Was it right?
How do you stay on top of on-chain trends?

name: DeFi Analyst/Token Researcher
oneLiner: Deep dive into DeFi protocols, tokenomics, and investment opportunities. Find alpha in the plumbing.
dayInTheLife: Analyze new DeFi protocol's smart contracts. Run tokenomics models. Check GitHub activity. Assess team credentials. Research governance risks. Write a detailed report. Present findings to fund. Debate risks with team. Monitor protocol updates. Track KPIs weekly.

juniorToSenior.junior: Learning DeFi mechanics, understanding protocols, writing analysis. Supervised research.
juniorToSenior.mid: Identifying protocol risks, modeling tokenomics, advising on investments. Published research.
juniorToSenior.senior: Protocol expert, fund advisor, respected analyst. Speaking at conferences. Alpha generator.

proofOfWorkTips: Publish detailed DeFi protocol analysis - Substack is good
Call out protocol risks early and publicly - be right
Model tokenomics for new projects - share spreadsheets
Write research reports and publish them
Build a following for your insights

avgCompRange.note: Hedge funds pay top dollar. Bounties and consulting add 30-50%.

interviewQuestions: Analyze a DeFi protocol for us. What's your investment thesis?
How do you think about tokenomics risk?
Tell us about a DeFi protocol you got wrong.
Design a hypothetical DeFi primitive.
What's an underrated DeFi protocol?

name: Community Manager
oneLiner: Build and nurture engaged communities on Discord, Twitter, and events. You're the vibe keeper.
dayInTheLife: Start community standup. Moderate Discord for 2 hours - ban 3 bots, answer questions. Plan weekly Twitter Space. Meet with core community members. Create memes. Moderate drama respectfully. Host AMA with founder. Analyze sentiment. Plan next month's events.

juniorToSenior.junior: Managing Discord, answering questions, enforcing rules. Building relationships with members.
juniorToSenior.mid: Leading community strategy, hosting events, building sub-communities. 10k+ active members.
juniorToSenior.senior: Building global community, influencing product decisions, mentoring. Community is a moat.

proofOfWorkTips: Build a thriving Discord from scratch - show growth and engagement metrics
Create viral Twitter threads about your project - engagement proof
Host successful events (AMAs, calls, competitions) - show attendance
Manage community during crisis - show maturity and skill
Document your community strategy and results

avgCompRange.note: Often includes community treasury allocation. Engagement bonuses common.

interviewQuestions: Tell us about a community crisis you managed.
How do you measure community health?
Design a community onboarding experience.
What's your philosophy on moderation?
Show us a community you built or managed.

name: Marketing Specialist
oneLiner: Promote projects, handle SEO, content, and growth hacking strategies for Web3 startups.
dayInTheLife: Plan weekly content calendar. Write 3 blog posts optimized for SEO. Create Twitter thread strategy. Analyze engagement metrics. Test messaging variants. Coordinate with community team. Plan launch campaign. Review competitor strategy. Report metrics to leadership.

juniorToSenior.junior: Writing content, managing social media, learning SEO. Supporting campaigns.
juniorToSenior.mid: Leading marketing strategy, creating content pillars, managing campaigns. Data-driven decisions.
juniorToSenior.senior: Leading growth, advising product, building brand. Thought leader in space.

proofOfWorkTips: Write published articles - Medium, Substack, dev platforms
Grow Twitter following with engaging, valuable content
Launch successful marketing campaigns - show metrics
Create viral social content - engagement matters
Document case studies of marketing wins

avgCompRange.note: Performance bonuses based on growth metrics common.

interviewQuestions: Tell us about a successful marketing campaign you led.
How would you position a new DeFi protocol?
Design a growth strategy for a crypto project.
How do you approach SEO for Web3?
What's your content strategy?

name: UI/UX Designer
oneLiner: Design intuitive interfaces for dApps, wallets, and DeFi protocols that users love.
dayInTheLife: User research interviews with traders. Design wallet flow. Create high-fidelity mockups in Figma. Test designs with users. Iterate based on feedback. Work with devs on handoff. Design system updates. Accessibility audits. Review competitor dApps.

juniorToSenior.junior: Creating component designs, learning user research, building design systems.
juniorToSenior.mid: Leading product design, conducting research, mentoring junior designers. 5+ shipped projects.
juniorToSenior.senior: Design director, setting design strategy, influencing product. Design-led growth.

proofOfWorkTips: Build a portfolio of shipped dApp designs
Create case studies explaining your design decisions
Contribute to open-source design systems (Chakra, etc)
Write about Web3 UX patterns and problems
Win design competitions or awards

avgCompRange.note: Senior designers and design leads earn more.

interviewQuestions: Walk us through your design process for a complex interface.
How do you approach UX for wallet/DeFi apps?
Show us your best design work and explain decisions.
How do you handle feedback and iterations?
Design a UI for [complex DeFi scenario].

name: NFT Artist/Designer
oneLiner: Create digital art, game assets, and immersive experiences for NFTs and metaverse projects.
dayInTheLife: Create 5 new NFT designs. Iterate on feedback. Render 3D assets in Blender. Check mint analytics. Engage collectors on Discord. Update roadmap visuals. Collaborate with dev on game assets. Post sketches on Twitter. Auction limited edition piece.

juniorToSenior.junior: Creating digital art, learning NFT design, minting small collections. Building portfolio.
juniorToSenior.mid: Leading art direction, creating collections, building collector community. 1000+ holders.
juniorToSenior.senior: Respected artist, influencing culture, mentoring artists. Art is investment-grade.

proofOfWorkTips: Mint NFT collections - sell them and show revenue
Get featured on OpenSea/Foundation - get sales
Build a collector community - engagement matters
Create culturally significant art to the community
Collaborate with known projects or artists

avgCompRange.note: Highly variable. Top artists earn 6-7 figures. Revenue share with projects common.

interviewQuestions: Show us your best work. What's the story behind it?
How do you approach an NFT collection?
What makes good NFT art?
Tell us about your collector community.
What's your vision for NFTs?

name: Product Manager
oneLiner: Define roadmaps, user needs, and strategy for Web3 products that users actually want.
dayInTheLife: User interviews with power traders. Analyze usage data and find friction points. Draft PRD for new feature. Prioritize roadmap. Review wireframes with design. Brainstorm solutions with engineers. Present strategy to leadership. Monitor launch metrics. Iterate based on feedback.

juniorToSenior.junior: Analyzing user feedback, supporting PRDs, learning product thinking. Junior features.
juniorToSenior.mid: Leading product strategy, running user research, driving launches. Multiple shipped features.
juniorToSenior.senior: VP Product, setting product vision, advising CEO. Product-market fit expert.

proofOfWorkTips: Contribute to open-source DeFi projects as product advisor
Write product strategy posts - Medium articles matter
Launch features and share the metrics
Show user research and feedback loops
Build a reputation for shipping

avgCompRange.note: VP/Head of Product earns 50%+ more. Equity important.

interviewQuestions: Tell us about a product you shipped. What was the impact?
How would you approach [specific DeFi problem]?
Walk us through your user research process.
How do you prioritize features?
What DeFi product would you build?

name: Business Development / Sales
oneLiner: Forge partnerships, drive sales, and build ecosystem relationships for crypto projects.
dayInTheLife: Call 10 potential partners. Pitch your protocol's integrations. Negotiate deal terms. Close partnership with exchange. Attend crypto conference for networking. Follow up on leads. Report on pipeline. Plan partnership announcements. Attend industry events.

juniorToSenior.junior: Prospecting, following up, learning sales process. Initial partnerships.
juniorToSenior.mid: Closing deals, managing partnerships, leading negotiations. 5+ major deals/year.
juniorToSenior.senior: VP BD, strategic partnerships, board-level relationships. Setting ecosystem strategy.

proofOfWorkTips: Show number of partnerships closed and their impact
Document deal processes and learnings
Build LinkedIn presence and network publicly
Speak at events about partnerships
Create partnerships playbooks

avgCompRange.note: Often includes commission on deals. High performers earn 50%+ more.

interviewQuestions: Tell us about your biggest partnership win.
How would you approach a partnership with [major platform]?
Walk us through your sales process.
How do you identify partnership opportunities?
What's your approach to negotiation?

name: Operations / Legal / Compliance
oneLiner: Handle operations, regulatory compliance, and legal/risk management for Web3 firms.
dayInTheLife: Review regulatory changes. Update compliance policies. Audit operational procedures. Meet with legal team. Risk assessment on new feature. Prepare compliance report. Handle regulatory inquiries. Train team on compliance. Manage insurance.

juniorToSenior.junior: Learning compliance, supporting policies, handling admin tasks.
juniorToSenior.mid: Leading compliance strategy, managing risk, driving operational efficiency.
juniorToSenior.senior: Chief Compliance Officer, setting company policy, board reporting. Legal authority.

proofOfWorkTips: Write detailed compliance documentation
Obtain compliance certifications (SOC 2, ISO, etc)
Speak at events about crypto compliance
Contribute to regulatory discussions
Build compliance frameworks

avgCompRange.note: Chief Compliance Officer roles pay premium.

interviewQuestions: How would you approach compliance for [jurisdiction]?
Tell us about a compliance challenge you solved.
What are key crypto compliance risks?
Design a compliance framework for a DeFi protocol.
How do you stay updated on regulations?

name: Economy Designer / Tokenomics
oneLiner: Design tokenomics, game economies, and DAO governance mechanisms that align incentives.
dayInTheLife: Model different tokenomics scenarios. Run simulations on token distribution. Design DAO voting mechanism. Analyze game theory incentives. Present model to team. Iterate based on feedback. Document tokenomics. Plan token launch strategy.

juniorToSenior.junior: Learning tokenomics, analyzing models, supporting design. Spreadsheet modeling.
juniorToSenior.mid: Designing tokenomics, running simulations, advising on incentives. Novel mechanisms.
juniorToSenior.senior: Tokenomics authority, designing games, speaking at conferences. Published research.

proofOfWorkTips: Publish tokenomics design posts - Medium is great
Create detailed tokenomics models - GitHub repos matter
Contribute to DAO governance design
Run public simulations and publish results
Speak about tokenomics at conferences

avgCompRange.note: Specialists in game theory and economics earn premium.

interviewQuestions: Design tokenomics for [specific protocol type].
How do you prevent token dumping after launch?
Tell us about a tokenomics design you're proud of.
How do you model incentive alignment?
What's the biggest tokenomics challenge in crypto?

name: Content Creator / Copywriter
oneLiner: Produce blogs, videos, and social content that educates and engages the Web3 community.
dayInTheLife: Write 2 blog posts on DeFi trends. Create Twitter thread strategy. Film YouTube video explaining protocol. Edit and publish. Engage with audience comments. Analyze engagement metrics. Plan content calendar. Promote content on socials.

juniorToSenior.junior: Writing blog posts, creating social content, learning storytelling.
juniorToSenior.mid: Leading content strategy, creating multimedia content, building audience. 10k+ followers.
juniorToSenior.senior: Thought leader, influencing opinion, leading content team. 100k+ audience.

proofOfWorkTips: Build audience on Twitter/Substack - show growth
Create viral posts - engagement metrics matter
Produce video content - YouTube channel valuable
Write published articles on major platforms
Build a personal brand and following

avgCompRange.note: Creators with 50k+ followers negotiate equity/revenue share.

interviewQuestions: Tell us about your best piece of content. Why did it work?
How do you approach explaining complex topics?
Design a content strategy for a crypto project.
What makes Web3 content different?
Show us your audience and engagement metrics.
